toxic_risk_screening_analysis.narrative:
TOXIC RISK SCREENING ANALYSIS
IS 
 
A risk screen is not required for this application.  There is no increase in the permitted emission rate of any toxin associated with this application. 
  
Permit Evaluation and Statement of Basis:  Site B5728 & B5729, Tesoro Refining and Marketing Company, Avon 
Refinery 150 Solano Way and 1750 Marina Vista Way, Martinez, CA  94553 
 
 
 171  
 BACT  There is no increase in SO2 emissions associated with changing the primary fuel source for S-916 and S-917 from 40# Fuel Gas to 100# Fuel Gas.  Based on the data available, there is less TRS in 100# Fuel Gas than there is in 40# Fuel Gas.  Pursuant to this app lication through permit conditions, the Di strict will impose a limit on the daily 
maximum TRS content of the fuel gas fired at S-916 and S-917 and the annual average of the daily TRS sample results.  The limit on the annual average TR S content of fuel fired at S-916 and S-917 will be set equivalent to what 
available data shows is the average of sample data ava ilable for the 40# Fuel Gas.  The daily maximum limit will be 
set equivalent to what available data shows is highest daily value on TRS or hydrogen sulfide in the 40# Fuel Gas. 
 The condition text will read as follows:  
3.     Permittee/Owner/Operator shall ensure that the TRS content of the Fuel Gas to be fired at S-916 and/or S-917 
is NOT greater than 300 ppmvd.  This condition will have been violated when the re sult of any daily laboratory 
analysis of the TRS content of the Fuel Gas to be fired at S-916 and/or S-917 is greater than 300 ppmvd. (basis: cumulative increase, BACT, offsets, Regulation 2-1-403)  4.     Permittee/Owner/Operator shall ensure that annual av erage of the daily Fuel Gas sample TRS analysis results 
is NOT greater than 281 ppmvd.  This condition will have b een violated when the annual average of the daily Fuel 
Gas sample TRS analysis results is greater than 281 ppm vd.  Permittee/Owner/Operator shall determine the annual 
average of the daily Fuel Gas sample TRS analysis resu lts by summing the TRS analysis results of each day during 
each rolling 52 consecutive week period, a nd dividing the sum by the number of days of sample analysis results. 
(basis: cumulative increase, BACT, offsets, Regulation 2-1-403)TOXIC RISK SCREENING ANALYSIS

findings:
STATEMENT OF COMPLIANCE
ANCE 
 
S-916 and S-917 should each comply with  Regulation 6 Particulate Matter and Visible Emi ssions.  Visible 
emissions should not exceed the Ringelm ann 1 limitation in this regulation. 
 S-916 and S-917 should also comply with Re gulation 9, Rule 1, Sulfur Dioxide.  No liquid fuel is to be permitted 
for use at S-916 or S-917 and natural gas or 100# Fuel Gas sha ll be the only fuels authorized  for use at each source.  
Regulation 9, Rule 1, Section 302 limits  SO2 emissions to less than 300 ppm dry.  SOx emissions will be limited by 
conditions and monitoring imposed on th e fuel fired at each source.   
 S-916 and S-917 are subject to Regulation 9, Rule 10 Nitrogen Oxides and Carbon Monoxide from Boilers, Steam Generators and Process Heaters in Petrol eum Refineries.  Each source is expect ed to remain in compliance with the 
requirements of this rule.  S-917 is subject to the NSPS in 40 CFR 60.104 (a)(1) and S- 917 is expected to remain  in compliance with the 
requirements of this standard.  This project is considered to be ministerial under the District's CEQA Regulation 2-1-311 and therefore is not subject to CEQA review.  The engineering review for this  project requires only the application of standard permit 
conditions and standard emission factors and therefore is  not discretionary as defi ned by CEQA.  Permit Handbook 
chapter 2.1 sets forth the ministerial nature of this project. 
  
Permit Evaluation and Statement of Basis:  Site B5728 & B5729, Tesoro Refining and Marketing Company, Avon 
Refinery 150 Solano Way and 1750 Marina Vista Way, Martinez, CA  94553 
 
 
 172  
This project is not within 1000 feet of the outer boundary of the nearest public school and is therefore not subject to the public notification requirements of Regulation 2-1-412.  A toxics risk screening is not required for this application.  PSD, NSPS, and NESHAPS are not triggered.   CONDITIONS

blocks.heading: TESORO REFINING  AND  MARKETING COMPANY
blocks.text:
TESORO REFINING  AND  MARKETING COMPANY
APPLICATION #6820  - PLANT #14628  
 
Tesoro Refining and Marketing Company submitted permit application #6820 to obtain an  Accelerated Permit to 
Operate to change the type of refinery  fuel gas permitted for use as a fuel at each of the following two sources from 
40# Fuel Gas to 100# Fuel Gas.  Tesoro also want s to be permitted to burn natural gas at S-917. 
 
S-916 No. 16 Furnace – No. 1 HDS Heater; Firing: Refinery Fuel Gas, Natural Gas; Maximum 
Firing Rate: 55 MMBtu/hr 
 S-917 No. 17 Furnace – No. 1 HDS Prefractionato r Reboiler; Firing: Refinery Fuel Gas 
Maximum Firing Rate: 18 MMBtu/hr 
 
100# Fuel Gas and 40# Fuel Gas are the two kinds of refinery  fuel gas fired at the Tesoro refinery.  100# Fuel Gas 
is gas formed during refinery process operations such as  catalytic cracking at S-802, Fluid Catalytic Cracking Unit, and S-806, Coker Fluid Coking Unit.  The gas is treated at one of Tesoro’s Gas Plants including being bubbled through diethanolamine (DEA) to remove sulfurous compounds and then it is used as a fuel at various combustion 
sources throughout the refinery.  40# Fuel Gas is made up of approximately 75 volume percent 100# Fuel Gas with the balance of the 40# Fuel Gas being vapors recovered from throughout the Tesoro refinery’s vapor recovery 
systems A-12 and A-14.  These vapor recovery systems are used to capture VOCs from various tanks, oil water separators and loading or material transfer sources and the vapors are compressed to the No. 1 Gas Plant for 
processing.  Prior to submittal of permit application #6820, S-916 was fired exclusively on 40# Fuel  Gas and S-917 was fired 
exclusively on 40# Fuel Gas.  Pursuant  to permit application #6820, each of S- 916 and S-917 is to be authorized to 
fire 100# Fuel Gas as its primary fuel and S-917 is to be permitted to fire natural gas.   40# Fuel Gas will no longer 
be burned at these sources.  Natural gas is already perm itted for use at S-916 but it is not hooked up to a natural gas 
feed line yet.  Tesoro submitted this accelerated permit application because a fugitive leak was detected in the 40# 
Fuel Gas system and to minimize loss of operation, the 100# fuel gas needed to be used as the primary fuel at S-916 and S-917.  Both sources are currently being fired on 100# Fuel Gas.  Though Tesoro has routinely subjected to 40# Fuel Gas and 100# Fuel Gas to analysis to determine its hydrogen sulfide content to ensure compliance with the NSPS in 40 CFR 60 104 (a) (1), to which S-917 is subject and S-916 
is not, there has not been routine analysis of either fuel to determine its total reduced sulfur (TRS) content.  To complete permit application #6820, Tesoro subjected the 100# fuel gas and 40# fuel gas to laboratory analysis to determine its TRS content on October 30, 2003.  Prior to s ubmitting the results of the analysis of the October 30, 
2003 sampling, Tesoro submitted results of TRS analyses of the 40# fuel gas and 100# fuel gas sampled in 1994 on 
March 2, 3, and 4.  On January 12, 2004, Tesoro provi ded a graphical output of a hydrogen sulfide analyzer 
disclosing the hydrogen sulfide content of the 40# Fuel Ga s for the 184 day period from July 1, 2002 to January 1, 
2003.  The analyzer data shows numerous spikes in the hydrogen sulfide content of the 40# Fuel Gas as high as 300 ppm, the limit on the range of the analyzer .  Results of these analyses will be averaged for each fu el.  Any increase 
in the emission rate of SO2 associated with this application will be based on the difference between the average TRS content of the 40# fuel gas and 100# fuel gas, ba sed on the 1994 and 2003 sample results and the hydrogen 
sulfide data from the analyzer with all of the TRS being conve rted to SO2.  The analyzer data is being averaged in 
because it shows that the TRS content of the fuel must be at least as  high the hydrogen sulfide content. 
  
Permit Evaluation and Statement of Basis:  Site B5728 & B5729, Tesoro Refining and Marketing Company, Avon 
Refinery 150 Solano Way and 1750 Marina Vista Way, Martinez, CA  94553 
 
 
 167  
Based on the results of the test data and the limits data available, Tesoro is concerned that there is insufficient data regarding the TRS content of 40# Fuel Gas to enable Tesoro to determine the
 historical TRS content of the 100 # 
Fuel Gas or to choose an appropriate limit on the TRS content of the 100 # Fuel Gas.  The District needs to impose a limit and do so based on the data available.  The District will allow a 90 period from the date of permit issuance before the limit and the requi rement for daily sampling of the 100# Fuel Gas will take 
effect.  During this 90 day period, Tesoro may conduct sa mpling and analysis of the 100# Fuel Gas and/or the 40# 
Fuel Gas and may apply to amend permit conditions for S-916 and/or S-917.  There is a federally enforceable limit on the maximum fu el firing rate for S-916 and S-917 set forth in condition 
#16685, part 1.  The condition reads in pertinent part:

Pursuant to this app lication through permit conditions, the Di strict will impose a limit on the daily 
maximum TRS content of the fuel gas fired at S-916 and S-917 and the annual average of the daily TRS sample results.  The limit on the annual average TR S content of fuel fired at S-916 and S-917 will be set equivalent to what

unmapped_blocks.heading: TESORO REFINING  AND  MARKETING COMPANY
unmapped_blocks.text:
TESORO REFINING  AND  MARKETING COMPANY
APPLICATION #6820  - PLANT #14628  
 
Tesoro Refining and Marketing Company submitted permit application #6820 to obtain an  Accelerated Permit to 
Operate to change the type of refinery  fuel gas permitted for use as a fuel at each of the following two sources from 
40# Fuel Gas to 100# Fuel Gas.  Tesoro also want s to be permitted to burn natural gas at S-917. 
 
S-916 No. 16 Furnace – No. 1 HDS Heater; Firing: Refinery Fuel Gas, Natural Gas; Maximum 
Firing Rate: 55 MMBtu/hr 
 S-917 No. 17 Furnace – No. 1 HDS Prefractionato r Reboiler; Firing: Refinery Fuel Gas 
Maximum Firing Rate: 18 MMBtu/hr 
 
100# Fuel Gas and 40# Fuel Gas are the two kinds of refinery  fuel gas fired at the Tesoro refinery.  100# Fuel Gas 
is gas formed during refinery process operations such as  catalytic cracking at S-802, Fluid Catalytic Cracking Unit, and S-806, Coker Fluid Coking Unit.  The gas is treated at one of Tesoro’s Gas Plants including being bubbled through diethanolamine (DEA) to remove sulfurous compounds and then it is used as a fuel at various combustion 
sources throughout the refinery.  40# Fuel Gas is made up of approximately 75 volume percent 100# Fuel Gas with the balance of the 40# Fuel Gas being vapors recovered from throughout the Tesoro refinery’s vapor recovery 
systems A-12 and A-14.  These vapor recovery systems are used to capture VOCs from various tanks, oil water separators and loading or material transfer sources and the vapors are compressed to the No. 1 Gas Plant for 
processing.  Prior to submittal of permit application #6820, S-916 was fired exclusively on 40# Fuel  Gas and S-917 was fired 
exclusively on 40# Fuel Gas.  Pursuant  to permit application #6820, each of S- 916 and S-917 is to be authorized to 
fire 100# Fuel Gas as its primary fuel and S-917 is to be permitted to fire natural gas.   40# Fuel Gas will no longer 
be burned at these sources.  Natural gas is already perm itted for use at S-916 but it is not hooked up to a natural gas 
feed line yet.  Tesoro submitted this accelerated permit application because a fugitive leak was detected in the 40# 
Fuel Gas system and to minimize loss of operation, the 100# fuel gas needed to be used as the primary fuel at S-916 and S-917.  Both sources are currently being fired on 100# Fuel Gas.  Though Tesoro has routinely subjected to 40# Fuel Gas and 100# Fuel Gas to analysis to determine its hydrogen sulfide content to ensure compliance with the NSPS in 40 CFR 60 104 (a) (1), to which S-917 is subject and S-916 
is not, there has not been routine analysis of either fuel to determine its total reduced sulfur (TRS) content.  To complete permit application #6820, Tesoro subjected the 100# fuel gas and 40# fuel gas to laboratory analysis to determine its TRS content on October 30, 2003.  Prior to s ubmitting the results of the analysis of the October 30, 
2003 sampling, Tesoro submitted results of TRS analyses of the 40# fuel gas and 100# fuel gas sampled in 1994 on 
March 2, 3, and 4.  On January 12, 2004, Tesoro provi ded a graphical output of a hydrogen sulfide analyzer 
disclosing the hydrogen sulfide content of the 40# Fuel Ga s for the 184 day period from July 1, 2002 to January 1, 
2003.  The analyzer data shows numerous spikes in the hydrogen sulfide content of the 40# Fuel Gas as high as 300 ppm, the limit on the range of the analyzer .  Results of these analyses will be averaged for each fu el.  Any increase 
in the emission rate of SO2 associated with this application will be based on the difference between the average TRS content of the 40# fuel gas and 100# fuel gas, ba sed on the 1994 and 2003 sample results and the hydrogen 
sulfide data from the analyzer with all of the TRS being conve rted to SO2.  The analyzer data is being averaged in 
because it shows that the TRS content of the fuel must be at least as  high the hydrogen sulfide content. 
  
Permit Evaluation and Statement of Basis:  Site B5728 & B5729, Tesoro Refining and Marketing Company, Avon 
Refinery 150 Solano Way and 1750 Marina Vista Way, Martinez, CA  94553 
 
 
 167  
Based on the results of the test data and the limits data available, Tesoro is concerned that there is insufficient data regarding the TRS content of 40# Fuel Gas to enable Tesoro to determine the
 historical TRS content of the 100 # 
Fuel Gas or to choose an appropriate limit on the TRS content of the 100 # Fuel Gas.  The District needs to impose a limit and do so based on the data available.  The District will allow a 90 period from the date of permit issuance before the limit and the requi rement for daily sampling of the 100# Fuel Gas will take 
effect.  During this 90 day period, Tesoro may conduct sa mpling and analysis of the 100# Fuel Gas and/or the 40# 
Fuel Gas and may apply to amend permit conditions for S-916 and/or S-917.  There is a federally enforceable limit on the maximum fu el firing rate for S-916 and S-917 set forth in condition 
#16685, part 1.  The condition reads in pertinent part:
